g.data-narration: Welcome to Free Space Management - the foundation of efficient file system design.
g.text: Track which disk blocks are free and available for allocation

g.data-narration: Free space management determines how efficiently your disk stores files. Poor management wastes storage.
g.text: Efficient free space management directly impacts:

g.data-narration: Four main methods manage free space: Bit Vector, Linked List, Grouping, and Counting. Each has trade-offs.
g.text: Four Methods of Free Space Management

g.data-narration: Bit Vector uses one bit per disk block. Zero means free, one means allocated. Simple and efficient.
g.text: Each disk block represented by 1 bit: 0 = free, 1 = allocated

g.data-narration: Bitmap size equals disk size divided by eight times block size. A 1TB disk with 4KB blocks needs about 32MB.
Bitmaps excel at finding free blocks and contiguous space but require significant memory for large disks.

g.text: Simple • Efficient • Easy to find n contiguous blocks
Large for big disks • Must keep in memory

g.data-narration: Linked List method chains free blocks together using pointers stored within the free blocks themselves.
g.text: Free blocks are linked together using pointers stored inside the free blocks themselves

g.data-narration: Linked Lists use no extra space since pointers live in the free blocks. However, finding contiguous blocks is difficult.
Grouping stores addresses of multiple free blocks in the first free block. Last address points to another group.
g.text: First free block stores addresses of n free blocks. Last points to another such block.

g.data-narration: Counting stores pairs of start block and count for consecutive free blocks. Efficient for clustered allocations.
g.text: Store (start_block, count) pairs for runs of consecutive free blocks

g.data-narration: Compare methods: Bitmap finds contiguous easily but has overhead. Linked List is simple but hard to find contiguous.
Real systems combine methods. Keep structures in memory for speed and write to disk periodically with journaling.
Four key methods: Bitmap for speed, Linked List for space, Grouping combines benefits, Counting excels with SSDs.
This diagram covered all four free space management methods. Understanding these is essential for file system design.
g.text: Four methods with different trade-offs - choose based on your specific needs
ext2/3/4 use bitmaps • Modern systems often use hybrid approaches
